maintenanceAgency.agencyName: eMelbourne - The Encyclopedia of Melbourne Online

biogHist.abstract:
Bouverie Street runs from Grattan Street in the north to Victoria Street in the south. It was likely named after a street in London.
A number of Melbourne streets were renumbered in the late 1880s and it seems that Bouverie Street may have been one of them. A funeral notice in the Age [can I italicise this] 21 March 1889, p. 8 [can I link to the Trove item? http://nla.gov.au/nla.news-article196995283] notes that the funeral of James McMahon will leave his late residence, 57 old number, 85 new, Bouverie Street, Carlton.
Andrew May's book "Melbourne Street Life" notes the following: "An unsuccessful application was made to change the name of Carlton’s Bouverie Street in order to attract a better class of tenants, the street having ‘lost caste’ in the view of its property owners because of the occupation by undesirable classes". My notes suggest that there were 35 signatories to the petition, which was not granted. The complaints of the petitioners was that "The street in the past had many Tenements, occupied by very Undesirable persons … Consequently the street lost caste, and the values have fallen from a rental and selling point of view … If the name were altered, a better class of tenants would be induced to live there, being so close to the University and city. It should be a splendid residential street and the increased value of the properties would greatly benefit the city revenue".
The archival reference for this is VPRS 3181/870, 1901 File 715. The original should be checked and digitised.

resourceRelation.relationEntry: 'Bouverie Street', in eMelbourne - The Encyclopedia of Melbourne Online, School of Historical Studies, Department of History, The University of Melbourne
